topic: Budget, Grants, Parliamentary Committees and Financial Control
subtopic: Annual Financial Statement, charged expenditure, demands, cut motions, appropriation, finance and audit committees

content.issueStory: Legislative control of public money is a cycle: authorisation before spending, scrutiny during policy and audit after spending; passing the Budget is only one stage. The Annual Financial Statement distinguishes charged and voted expenditure. Lok Sabha votes demands for grants; appropriation authorises withdrawal from the Consolidated Fund; finance legislation gives effect to taxation. Committees deepen scrutiny beyond floor time.
content.coreConcept: The Annual Financial Statement distinguishes charged and voted expenditure. Lok Sabha votes demands for grants; appropriation authorises withdrawal from the Consolidated Fund; finance legislation gives effect to taxation. Committees deepen scrutiny beyond floor time.
dimensions: Budget documents classify receipts, expenditure and fiscal proposals within constitutional funds.
Charged expenditure is discussed but not voted, while voted expenditure proceeds through demands for grants.
Cut motions test policy, economy or a specific grievance under House rules.
Appropriation Bill authorises withdrawal from Consolidated Fund after grants.
Public Accounts Committee examines CAG reports and expenditure regularity; Estimates Committee examines economy and efficiency.
Committee on Public Undertakings examines public enterprises within its mandate.
content.constitutionalLink: Articles 112-117, 265-267 and 148-151; Lok Sabha financial procedure and committee rules.
content.dataReport: Uploaded source basis: STATIC_09_Polity.pdf and PYQ_06_Polity_Governance.pdf. Refresh amendments, statutory rules, office status and later judgments from official sources: Constitution of India, Union Budget, CAG and Parliament committee portals.
content.caseStudy: CAG audits and reports; PAC conducts legislative examination. Neither is the executive department that originally spent the money.
content.pyqLink: UPSC patterns test Article-institution matches, exceptions, procedure and comparative statements within Budget, Grants, Parliamentary Committees and Financial Control. Resolve each statement through source, scope, procedure and consequence.

answerFramework.intro: Introduce Budget, Grants, Parliamentary Committees and Financial Control through the controlling constitutional principle and legal source.
body: Map the authority: Articles 112-117, 265-267 and 148-151; Lok Sabha financial procedure and committee rules.

Explain the first-order design: Budget documents classify receipts, expenditure and fiscal proposals within constitutional funds. Charged expenditure is discussed but not voted, while voted expenditure proceeds through demands for grants. Cut motions test policy, economy or a specific grievance under House rules.

Analyse safeguards and tensions: Appropriation Bill authorises withdrawal from Consolidated Fund after grants. Public Accounts Committee examines CAG reports and expenditure regularity; Estimates Committee examines economy and efficiency. Committee on Public Undertakings examines public enterprises within its mandate.

Use CAG audits and reports; PAC conducts legislative examination. Neither is the executive department that originally spent the money. and close with constitutional accountability plus dated legal verification.
answerFramework.conclusion: A strong Polity answer joins constitutional text, institutional purpose, judicial doctrine and a workable accountability safeguard.

mainsExamples: Demand for grant voted by Lok Sabha.
prelimsFacts: Annual Financial Statement is under Article 112.
Rajya Sabha does not vote demands for grants.
Charged expenditure is not submitted to vote.
Appropriation is needed for withdrawal from Consolidated Fund.
CAG and PAC have distinct roles.
Estimates Committee focuses on estimates and efficiency.

revisionBox.prompt: What is the controlling rule in Budget, Grants, Parliamentary Committees and Financial Control?
revisionBox.answer: The Annual Financial Statement distinguishes charged and voted expenditure. Lok Sabha votes demands for grants; appropriation authorises withdrawal from the Consolidated Fund; finance legislation gives effect to taxation. Committees deepen scrutiny beyond floor time.

revisionBox.prompt: What is the primary constitutional or legal authority?
revisionBox.answer: Articles 112-117, 265-267 and 148-151; Lok Sabha financial procedure and committee rules.

revisionBox.prompt: What is the fastest elimination trap?
revisionBox.answer: Do not say PAC conducts the original audit.

revisionBox.prompt: Where should later legal changes be verified?
revisionBox.answer: Constitution of India, Union Budget, CAG and Parliament committee portals

story: Legislative control of public money is a cycle: authorisation before spending, scrutiny during policy and audit after spending; passing the Budget is only one stage.
core_concept: The Annual Financial Statement distinguishes charged and voted expenditure. Lok Sabha votes demands for grants; appropriation authorises withdrawal from the Consolidated Fund; finance legislation gives effect to taxation. Committees deepen scrutiny beyond floor time.

mindmap_json.center: Budget, Grants, Parliamentary Committees and Financial Control

branches.label: Text
children: Budget documents classify receipts, expenditure and fiscal proposals within constitutional funds.
Charged expenditure is discussed but not voted, while voted expenditure proceeds through demands for grants.

branches.label: Institution
children: Cut motions test policy, economy or a specific grievance under House rules.
Appropriation Bill authorises withdrawal from Consolidated Fund after grants.

branches.label: Safeguard
children: Public Accounts Committee examines CAG reports and expenditure regularity; Estimates Committee examines economy and efficiency.
Committee on Public Undertakings examines public enterprises within its mandate.

branches.label: Traps
children: Do not say PAC conducts the original audit.
Do not give Rajya Sabha power to vote demands for grants.
Do not confuse Finance Bill with Appropriation Bill.

key_facts: Annual Financial Statement is under Article 112.
Rajya Sabha does not vote demands for grants.
Charged expenditure is not submitted to vote.
Appropriation is needed for withdrawal from Consolidated Fund.
CAG and PAC have distinct roles.
Estimates Committee focuses on estimates and efficiency.
common_traps: Do not say PAC conducts the original audit.
Do not give Rajya Sabha power to vote demands for grants.
Do not confuse Finance Bill with Appropriation Bill.
